purpose: Per-fixture root-cause audit + per-language tree-sitter node-kind audit before plan authoring.

extended_by: vuln-migration-v1 M3 (PHP echo_statement / print_intrinsic special-case at L3941-3983)
field_access_info-extension-v1 M5 (OCaml application_expression special-case at L3989-4016)

current_invocation_sites.site: detect_sources_ast at taint.rs:4201 — fallback chain after find_parent_assignment_var + extract_assigned_var
current_invocation_sites.purpose: regex-free fallback for sources whose tainted data is delivered by-pointer in the first call argument (e.g., C `fgets(buf, ..., stdin)`)

current_invocation_sites.site: detect_sinks_ast at taint.rs:4316 — fallback chain after regex_patterns extract_call_arg / extract_sink_var_from_statement
current_invocation_sites.purpose: regex-free fallback for call-shaped sinks; walks descendant's `arguments` list

current_helper_behavior.summary: Walks descendant's `arguments` field (or first arg-list child) and returns the first NAMED, non-string-literal child whose text head (after split on '.', strip leading & or $) is a valid identifier.
current_helper_behavior.string_literal_filter: string_kinds = string_node_kinds(language) — child kinds matching string_literal / string / template_string etc are skipped (taint.rs:4048-4050).
current_helper_behavior.non_named_skip: child.is_named() == false skipped (taint.rs:4044-4046) — punctuation tokens like '(', ',', ')'.
current_helper_behavior.identifier_validation: is_valid_identifier(head) — text trimmed via split('.').next() then trim_start_matches('&').trim_start_matches('$').
current_helper_behavior.does_NOT_descend: If the first named child is itself a call_expression / object_creation_expression / new_expression / declaration / binary_expression, the helper takes node_text(child).split('.').next() — which yields literals like 'new' (Java) or 'std' (Cpp) or the receiver of the inner constructor — not the underlying tainted variable inside the inner constructor's argument list. is_valid_identifier might OR MIGHT NOT accept the synthetic — typically yields a wrong identifier or the keyword `new` (Java) which is not a valid identifier and is skipped; helper returns None. The whole descendant is dropped at taint.rs:4232 (`if let Some(var) = var`).

java_deserialization_positive.fixture_lines: 9-line file; tainted line is L7: `new java.io.ObjectInputStream(new java.io.ByteArrayInputStream(d.getBytes())).readObject();`
java_deserialization_positive.source_at_L6: request.getParameter("d") — assigns to local var `d` (HttpParam source already detected, GREEN at sink propagation step)
java_deserialization_positive.sink_descendant: object_creation_expression for `new java.io.ObjectInputStream(...)` — call_node_kinds(Java) includes object_creation_expression; matched via raw-substring fallback `('', 'new java.io.ObjectInputStream(')` (taint.rs:2375).
java_deserialization_positive.current_helper_outcome: First named child of arguments list is itself `object_creation_expression`. Its text is `new java.io.ByteArrayInputStream(d.getBytes())`. node_text.split('.').next() → `new java`. trim_start_matches('&'/'$') → `new java`. is_valid_identifier(`new java`) → false (contains space). Helper returns None.
java_deserialization_positive.downstream_effect: var fallback chain at taint.rs:4298-4338 returns None for sink → TaintSink not emitted → flow analysis sees no sink for d → integ test asserts ≥1 finding → RED.
java_deserialization_positive.fix_shape: When first named child is `object_creation_expression`, descend INTO its arguments-field and recurse. Goal: reach `d.getBytes()` (a method_invocation), then split('.').next() → 'd'. Identifier valid → return Some("d").

scala_deserialization_positive.fixture_lines: 7-line file; tainted line is L4: `new java.io.ObjectInputStream(new java.io.ByteArrayInputStream(d.getBytes)).readObject()`
scala_deserialization_positive.source_at_L3: request.getQueryString("d").get — already detected per M2 GREEN.
scala_deserialization_positive.sink_descendant: Scala tree-sitter parses `new java.io.ObjectInputStream(...)` as `instance_expression` wrapping a `call_expression`, OR as a `call_expression` with the function-position child being `new java.io.ObjectInputStream`. call_node_kinds(Scala) = ["call_expression"]. The sink is matched via raw-substring fallback `('', 'new java.io.ObjectInputStream(')` (taint.rs:3268).
scala_deserialization_positive.first_arg_node: Inside the outer call's arguments — the inner `new java.io.ByteArrayInputStream(d.getBytes)` expression (likely also call_expression-wrapping-instance-expression; or a `creator` / `instance_expression` node depending on grammar version).
scala_deserialization_positive.current_helper_outcome: Same as Java: first named child is a nested constructor call; node_text yields `new java.io.ByteArrayInputStream(d.getBytes)` whose split('.').next() = `new java` — NOT a valid identifier. Returns None.
scala_deserialization_positive.downstream_effect: Same as Java — sink dropped, RED.
scala_deserialization_positive.fix_shape: Detect nested-call/instance/object-creation node as first arg → descend into its arguments → return identifier from the inner call's first arg.

cpp_deserialization_positive.fixture_lines: 8-line file; tainted line is L7: `boost::archive::text_iarchive ia(std::stringstream(d) >> obj);`
cpp_deserialization_positive.source_at_L6: std::getenv("D") — EnvVar source. M2 added `std::getenv` to CPP_AST_SOURCES call_names (taint.rs:2570 region) → source IS detected per M2-report L88 (`Source side now detected`).
cpp_deserialization_positive.sink_descendant_HYPOTHESIS_A: tree-sitter-cpp parses `boost::archive::text_iarchive ia(...)` as a `declaration` node containing a `function_declarator` for `ia(...)` — NOT a call_expression. call_node_kinds(Cpp) = ["call_expression"], so the call-shape path doesn't fire. Sink could only match via raw-substring fallback in member_patterns_match — but member_patterns_match is invoked per-descendant, and the per-descendant text at the function_declarator level may or may not contain the receiver substring `boost::archive::text_iarchive`.
cpp_deserialization_positive.sink_descendant_HYPOTHESIS_B: tree-sitter-cpp may parse this as a `call_expression` whose function is the qualified identifier `boost::archive::text_iarchive` and whose `ia(...)` part is misparsed. In that case, extract_call_name returns the FQN, member_patterns rfind('.') split fails (no `.` in `::`-separated name) → fall through to raw-substring `('', 'boost::archive::text_iarchive')` in CPP_AST_SINKS:2647 — which matches descendant_text.contains. Then var-extraction is invoked on the call_expression — but the first arg is `std::stringstream(d) >> obj` (a binary expression `>>`), and `std::stringstream(d)` is a nested call_expression whose first arg IS the identifier `d`.
cpp_deserialization_positive.M2_report_finding: M2-report.json L91 reports: 'Sink (boost::archive::text_iarchive) still empirically returns sinks=[]. ...likely a tree-sitter-cpp parsing variance distinct from the FQN substring path.' This points toward Hypothesis A (sink does not even fire — different from Java/Scala which fire-but-var-extract-blocks).
cpp_deserialization_positive.fix_shape_A: If Hypothesis A: cpp_deserialization_positive needs a DIFFERENT fix — either add `declaration` to call_node_kinds(Cpp) for sink-detection (tightly scoped to deserialization-style constructor declarations) OR add a CPP_AST_SINKS member_patterns entry that's a sink-detection extension. M1 of this milestone MUST verify the AST shape via a small inspection (e.g., a transient unit test or `tldr structure` on the fixture) before settling on the fix.
cpp_deserialization_positive.fix_shape_B: If Hypothesis B: same nested-constructor descent as Java/Scala — first arg is `std::stringstream(d) >> obj` binary_expression; helper must (i) descend into binary_expression's left child and (ii) descend into the inner call_expression's first arg → reach `d`.
cpp_deserialization_positive.investigation_decision: M1 of this plan SHALL verify the cpp shape empirically and choose the right fix. If Hypothesis A turns out to be the case AND the cpp fix requires a non-additive extension to call_node_kinds(Cpp) or a separate cpp-specific code path, the cpp fixture MAY be deferred to a future milestone (cpp-deser-declaration-v1) and only java+scala closed by this milestone. This is documented as a risk in the plan's premortem; it would lower the closes-3 to closes-2 and surface 1 carry-forward.

java.verification_source: tree-sitter-java grammar; confirmed via call_node_kinds(Java) at ast_utils.rs:23
java.shape_for_new_X(args): object_creation_expression { type: type_identifier or scoped_type_identifier, arguments: argument_list }
java.extract_call_name_java_returns: `new java.io.ObjectInputStream` for the outer; `new java.io.ByteArrayInputStream` for the inner (per ast_utils.rs:668-672)
java.implications_for_helper: The helper's existing `descendant.child_by_field_name('arguments')` works for the outer object_creation_expression. The first named child of `arguments` is the INNER object_creation_expression. The helper must recognize that and descend (recurse) into the inner's `arguments` field.

scala.verification_source: tree-sitter-scala grammar; pinned in tldr-core Cargo.toml. call_node_kinds(Scala) = ['call_expression']; instance_expression is NOT in call_node_kinds — so for instance_expression the helper would not be invoked unless the AST puts the call_expression at the outer node and instance_expression underneath.
scala.shape_for_new_X(args): Likely call_expression whose `function` field is `instance_expression` { 'new' keyword + class type } and whose `arguments` field is argument_list. Verify in M1.
scala.extract_call_name_scala_returns: node_text of `child_by_field_name('function').or_else(child(0))` — for `new java.io.ObjectInputStream(...)` likely returns `new java.io.ObjectInputStream` (the instance_expression text including the `new` keyword).
scala.implications_for_helper: If the outer is call_expression, helper enters `arguments` correctly. First named child of `arguments` is the INNER call_expression / instance_expression for `new java.io.ByteArrayInputStream(d.getBytes)`. Recursive descent must handle BOTH `call_expression` and `instance_expression` node-kind families. M1 inspection required to pin down exact node shape.

node_kinds_for_constructor_invocation: new_expression (heap allocation, not used in this fixture)
cpp.child_field_for_args: arguments (call_expression / new_expression); function_declarator's parameters field for declaration
cpp.verification_source: tree-sitter-cpp grammar; `new T(args)` parses as new_expression { type, arguments }; `T t(args)` parses as declaration { ... function_declarator { declarator: identifier, parameters: parameter_list } }
cpp.shape_for_T_id_args_in_fixture: `boost::archive::text_iarchive ia(std::stringstream(d) >> obj);` — likely `declaration` with function_declarator. The `ia(std::stringstream(d) >> obj)` looks like a function-prototype shape to tree-sitter — this is the C++ 'most vexing parse' surface.
cpp.implications_for_helper: If declaration shape: helper not invoked (descendant kind isn't call_expression). Sink-pattern matching also doesn't fire via call-shape path. The fix may NOT be in extract_first_identifier_arg_ast at all — it may be at the sink-pattern detection layer (M2-report's tree-sitter-cpp parsing variance). Investigation in M1 MUST resolve this. If shape is call_expression: helper IS invoked, and the first arg is binary_expression (`std::stringstream(d) >> obj`) — recursive descent must handle binary_expression too (left child is a call_expression for `std::stringstream(d)`).

string_literal_regression_guard.concern: When descending through nested constructor args, may we pick up a string literal inside the constructor and treat it as a tainted var?
string_literal_regression_guard.recursive_descent_implication: If we add recursive descent into nested call/object_creation/new nodes, the filter must apply at EVERY recursion level, not just the outermost. Concretely: `new ObjectInputStream("hardcoded")` — when descending, we should NOT pick up `"hardcoded"` as a var. Current filter handles this if applied per-arg-list-child at every level.
string_literal_regression_guard.regression_fixture_evidence: Existing FP fixtures (cpp/java/scala/deserialization_string_literal_fp.{ext}) wrap constructor names in string literals at the outer `let s = "...new java.io.ObjectInputStream..."` level — those are caught by is_in_string at the descendant filter (taint.rs:4262), upstream of helper invocation. Internal string-literal in constructor arg (e.g., `new ObjectInputStream("file.ser")`) is a separate case and string_node_kinds covers it at every recursion level.
string_literal_regression_guard.verdict: Regression-guard preserved as long as string_node_kinds filter is applied at every recursion level.

recursion_depth_concern.fixture_evidence: Java/Scala fixtures have 2 levels of nesting (outer ObjectInputStream → inner ByteArrayInputStream → method_invocation `d.getBytes()`).
recursion_depth_concern.future_proofing: A fix that handles only 1-level descent closes the 3 fixtures but is brittle for chains like `new A(new B(new C(d)))` — 3+ level. Recommended: bounded recursion (e.g., max depth 4 or 5) with cycle protection. Alternatively, BFS over the descendant subtree seeking the first non-string identifier-shaped leaf within a depth budget.
recursion_depth_concern.BFS_alternative: PHP echo_statement special-case at taint.rs:3954-3982 already uses BFS over named descendants (with string-skip). The Java/Scala/Cpp case can mirror this pattern: when the helper's primary loop encounters a child whose kind is in the 'descend through' set (object_creation_expression / new_expression / call_expression / instance_expression / binary_expression), enter a BFS over that child's named descendants seeking the first identifier-leaf, with string_kinds skip applied. This avoids unbounded recursion and naturally handles chains of arbitrary depth.

related_helper_invocation_sites_concern.concern: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast is invoked at TWO sites (taint.rs:4201 source detection; taint.rs:4316 sink detection). Modifying the helper changes BOTH paths. Could the change regress sources that currently work via the helper's first-identifier-only logic?
related_helper_invocation_sites_concern.audit: Source-side helper invocation is the LAST or near-last fallback for sources (after find_parent_assignment_var + extract_assigned_var). Most sources extract a var via parent-assignment (e.g., `String d = request.getParameter("d")` → `d`). The helper's role for sources is the C-style by-pointer pattern (`fgets(buf, ..., stdin)` → `buf`). Adding nested-constructor descent does not regress that case because C `fgets(buf, ...)` first arg `buf` is an identifier, not a nested constructor — the recursive descent only triggers when the first arg IS a constructor/call/instance node.
related_helper_invocation_sites_concern.regression_test_strategy: M1 RED capture must include the C `fgets`-style source fixture run to confirm pre-extension behavior is preserved. Additionally, run the full vuln_migration_v1_red suite at the M3 verification gate to detect any GREEN→RED transitions across all 158 currently-GREEN tests.

additive_vs_modifying_helper.verdict: MODIFYING (not purely additive). The helper's main loop body must be extended with conditional descent. Mitigated by: (1) descent only triggers when first named child kind is in a constructor-like set — non-constructor first-args are unchanged path; (2) extensive regression-test coverage at M3 verification (all 158 currently-GREEN tests must remain GREEN); (3) bounded recursion with explicit string-kind filter at every level.

loc_estimate.primary_extension: +30-50 LOC (new helper sub-function or BFS block in extract_first_identifier_arg_ast)
loc_estimate.regression_tests_if_needed: +0 LOC — relies entirely on the existing 3 RED tests as contract; no NEW RED tests authored. The 158-test sweep at M3 IS the regression guard.
loc_estimate.comments_and_doc_block: +10-20 LOC (per-language node-kind list + BFS rationale + string-kind filter rationale + recursion-bound rationale)
loc_estimate.total: +40-70 LOC, all in extract_first_identifier_arg_ast region (taint.rs:3934+) — no other source changes anticipated

milestone_decomposition_recommendation.M1_investigate_red_capture: +0 LOC source. Capture RED state of 3 fixtures pre-fix; confirm 158 currently-GREEN. Inspect cpp fixture's actual tree-sitter parse shape (resolve Hypothesis A vs B). If A: surface that cpp may be deferred and document scope reduction.
milestone_decomposition_recommendation.M2_implement_extension: +40-70 LOC source. Single small commit; not atomic. Extend extract_first_identifier_arg_ast with BFS-descent for constructor-like first-arg shapes across Java/Scala (and Cpp if Hypothesis B confirmed in M1).
milestone_decomposition_recommendation.M3_verify: +0 LOC source. Run vuln_migration_v1_red full suite; binary smoke test on 18 string_literal_fp fixtures; confirm 158 GREEN remain GREEN; 3 RED transition GREEN (or 2/3 if cpp deferred).
milestone_decomposition_recommendation.M4_changelog_tag: +20 LOC docs. CHANGELOG entry; local tag; NO push, NO publish, NO version bump.
milestone_decomposition_recommendation.rationale: Smaller than the 6-milestone field_access_info-extension-v1 precedent. No serialization concerns (single function in single file). No atomic commit needed (M2 is purely additive at the function level — descent only triggers for constructor-shaped first args).

tiger_risks_high_likelihood_or_high_impa: R1 — cpp Hypothesis A (parsing variance) means the helper extension does NOT close cpp_deserialization_positive. Likelihood: medium (per M2-report's tree-sitter-cpp variance hint). Impact: high — cpp carry-forward survives. Mitigation: M1 pre-investigation resolves this; if A confirmed, cpp scope drops to ~2/3 and a follow-on milestone is documented.

R2 — recursive descent picks up wrong identifier (e.g., reaches `getBytes` method-name instead of `d`). Likelihood: medium. Impact: high — wrong var means flow propagation is incorrect. Mitigation: BFS prefers identifier nodes at descent leaves; method_invocation node text starts with the receiver `d.getBytes()` whose split('.').next() yields `d` — correct. Verify with M3 integ test on java/scala fixtures.

R3 — descent regresses the C `fgets(buf, ..., stdin)` source extraction OR other call_expression first-arg extractions across the 158 GREEN. Likelihood: low (descent triggers only when first child kind ∈ constructor-like set; identifier-shaped first args bypass descent). Impact: high — GREEN→RED transitions. Mitigation: M3 runs full vuln_migration_v1_red suite + rr_framework_integ_test + test_e2e_in_security_vuln; gating threshold 158 GREEN must remain GREEN.

R4 — string_kinds filter not applied at recursion leaves causes string-literal regression on outer-string FP fixtures. Likelihood: low (filter explicitly applied at every level per design). Impact: high (closes-#24 regression). Mitigation: explicit unit + integration test; M3 binary smoke on all 18 string_literal_fp fixtures.
elephant_risks_low_likelihood_or_low_imp: E1 — Scala instance_expression node-kind difference between grammar versions. Likelihood: low. Impact: low (M1 inspection pins down current grammar's shape; document in helper comment).

E2 — Recursion depth limit accidentally too low for valid-but-deep chains. Likelihood: very low (real-world chains rarely exceed 3 levels). Impact: low (helper falls through to existing fallback chain).

E3 — Comment / documentation drift between helper extension and the per-language node-kind list. Likelihood: medium (large doc-comment blocks). Impact: low (cosmetic). Mitigation: CHANGELOG architectural-note explicitly cross-references taint.rs anchor lines.

stop_threshold_for_M3.primary_gate: vuln_migration_v1_red: pre-extension RED count 8 → post-extension RED count ≤5 (closes 3 Bucket B).
stop_threshold_for_M3.secondary_gate: If cpp Hypothesis A confirmed (M1 finding) and cpp deferred: vuln_migration_v1_red post-extension RED count ≤6 (closes 2 of 3); document as carry-forward. NOT a failure mode for the milestone — it's a scope reduction surfaced at M1.
stop_threshold_for_M3.regression_gate: All currently-GREEN tests across vuln_migration_v1_red, vuln_migration_v1_composite_red (1/1), rr_framework_integ_test (18/18), test_e2e_in_security_vuln (36/36) — MUST remain GREEN.
stop_threshold_for_M3.build_gates: cargo check --workspace PASS; cargo clippy --all-targets --workspace -- -D warnings PASS.

out_of_scope: Modifying call_node_kinds(Cpp) (would risk false positives across all cpp fixtures; out of this scope)
Adding new VulnType / TaintSinkType / TaintSourceType variants (none needed)
Modifying field_access_info or extract_call_name_* (no helper-extension precedent for those changes here)
Ruby/Cpp/Java/Scala source-bank or sink-bank ADDITIONS (vuln-source-parity-v1 closed those)
verification_method: Read taint.rs:3920-4065 (helper definition) ✓ VERIFIED; Read taint.rs:4180-4338 (invocation sites) ✓ VERIFIED; Read all 3 deserialization_positive fixtures ✓ VERIFIED; Read M5-carry-forward.json + M2-report.json ✓ VERIFIED; ast_utils.rs:18-36 (call_node_kinds) ✓ VERIFIED; ast_utils.rs:752-757 (extract_call_name_scala) ✓ VERIFIED; ast_utils.rs:654-675 (extract_call_name_java) ✓ VERIFIED; CPP_AST_SINKS at taint.rs:2618-2659 ✓ VERIFIED; member_patterns_match at taint.rs:3845-3920 ✓ VERIFIED.
